summary: Replaced the hardcoded INTEGRATION_LAST_REVIEWED rubber-stamp with a user-data-dir compat manifest (~/.aitri-hub/integration-compat.json) carrying reviewedUpTo + reviewedAt + changelogHash + reviewerNote. Added SHA-256 hashing of the per-version CHANGELOG section for drift detection, a new `aitri-hub integration review <version>` CLI subcommand that attests and persists reviews, and banner provenance (reviewed-at date + 12-char hash prefix) so the dashboard shows when/what was reviewed. FALLBACK_BASELINE (0.1.80) remains the single-source-of-truth fallback when the manifest is absent; INTEGRATION_LAST_REVIEWED is retained as an alias for backward compatibility.

files_modified.change: Added FALLBACK_BASELINE = '0.1.80' (single source of truth for pre-manifest fallback). Kept INTEGRATION_LAST_REVIEWED as alias of FALLBACK_BASELINE for backward compat. Added INTEGRATION_COMPAT_FILE and INTEGRATION_COMPAT_TMP_FILE for atomic writes.

Rewrote evaluateIntegrationAlert(detectedVersion, reviewedUpTo, opts) — explicit 2-arg baseline with optional {reviewedAt, changelogHash, currentChangelogHash}. Branches: undetectable (null detected), drift (changelogHash !== currentHash), version mismatch (semverGt). All alert payloads carry reviewedAt + changelogHash (null-or-value). Removed top-level import of INTEGRATION_LAST_REVIEWED (FR-033 AC-4 invariant).

collectAll() now reads the manifest via readManifest(), falls back to FALLBACK_BASELINE when absent/invalid, computes currentChangelogHash via readAndHashSection for drift detection, and passes all three into evaluateIntegrationAlert. Logs structured WARN when changelog not locatable — drift check is skipped, version-mismatch path still runs.
Added `integration` subcommand dispatch with `review` action. Updated USAGE string to document `aitri-hub integration review <version> [--changelog <path>] [--note <str>]`.

Added formatReviewedAt(iso) formatter (UTC 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm UTC'). Banner now renders a provenance subtext with reviewed-at date + 12-char hash prefix (both-or-neither invariant, per FR-035 AC-3). data-testid='integration-alert-provenance' for Playwright hooks.
Added .integration-alert-banner__provenance style (flex-basis 100%, 12px, monospace, dark subtext) for the new provenance subtext row.

Updated 18 call sites to the new 2-arg evaluateIntegrationAlert signature (detectedVersion, reviewedUpTo). Imports INTEGRATION_LAST_REVIEWED from constants and derives HIGHER via patch-bump to decouple from future bumps.

Updated evaluateIntegrationAlert('0.1.99') → evaluateIntegrationAlert('0.1.99', '0.1.80') to match the new 2-arg signature; E2E flow still verifies integrationAlert is embedded and round-trips via writeDashboard.
Bound the web server to 127.0.0.1 explicitly at server.listen(port, '127.0.0.1', …) to fix BG-001 (loopback-only listener — macOS firewall/privacy posture) unblocking verify-complete.

files_created.purpose: Reads/writes ~/.aitri-hub/integration-compat.json. readManifest() returns {status:'valid'|'absent', data} and never throws (logs WARN on malformed). writeManifest() validates schema (semver reviewedUpTo, ISO reviewedAt, 64-hex changelogHash, ≤500-char reviewerNote), writes via temp+rename atomicity, chmods 0600. compatManifestPath() exposes the absolute path for error messages. Does NOT create the hub dir on read (FR-030 AC-5).

resolveChangelogPath({override}) — 3-layer resolution (explicit override → bundled aitri core → throw ChangelogNotFound). Applies fs.realpathSync for symlink resolution. extractSection(changelog, version) — regex anchor on `## <version>` heading, trims trailing whitespace, throws SectionNotFound when missing. hashSection(body) — SHA-256 hex64 of trimmed body. readAndHashSection(version, opts) — convenience combiner returning {path, hash, body}.

cmdIntegrationReview(rest) — parses `<version> [--changelog <path>] [--note <str>]`, validates semver version + note ≤500 chars + --changelog no-null-byte, calls readAndHashSection to attest, applies a symlink-escape guard (realpath both sides before startsWith), writes the manifest, prints success with manifest path + 12-char hash prefix. Exit codes: OK=0, USAGE=1, INVALID_VERSION=2, CHANGELOG_NOT_FOUND=3, SECTION_NOT_FOUND=4.

test_results.notes: Feature's behavioral surface is covered by integration-guard.test.js (signature + drift + undetectable paths) and e2e-collector-cycle.test.js (writeDashboard round-trip with new integrationAlert payload shape). No new unit/integration test files were added — new branches fit the existing suites. Drift detection E2E (manifest + modified CHANGELOG round-trip) validated manually during Phase 5 verify-run; see 04_TEST_RESULTS.json for the cumulative tally.

environment_variables: AITRI_HUB_DIR (existing, optional override of ~/.aitri-hub — used by manifest read/write)
AITRI_HUB_DIR_OVERRIDE (existing, test-only alias consumed by dashboard store)

technical_debt.substitution: INTEGRATION_LAST_REVIEWED constant retained in lib/constants.js as an alias of FALLBACK_BASELINE for backward-compat with any downstream consumer. Not used by integration-guard.js (which now takes reviewedUpTo as an argument). Can be removed once a grep confirms zero external consumers.

CHANGELOG section hashing trims trailing whitespace only — intra-section whitespace edits (e.g. a reflow inside a bullet) will flip the hash and surface as drift. This is intentional (conservative false-positive bias) and matches the user's expectation that any content edit warrants a re-review, but may produce noise if the upstream CHANGELOG is reformatted mechanically. Revisit if drift false-positive rate exceeds 1 per review cycle.

Changelog resolution falls back to the bundled aitri core module — if the user runs the Hub outside a workspace where aitri is installed, --changelog must be supplied. Documented in the CLI error hint ('install the aitri CLI (`npm i -g aitri`) or pass --changelog <path>').
